mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database designed for the real-time web. It is ideal for applications requiring flexible data models and real-time capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the SurrealDB Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install SurrealDB using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official SurrealDB Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for SurrealDB to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/surrealdb/main/deploy/crds/surrealdbs.surrealdb.com_customresourcedefinition.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRD for SurrealDB.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB
steps.description:
This step installs the SurrealDB Helm chart into a specified namespace, deploying the database into your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --create-namespace --version 3.0.2

```

> Installs SurrealDB in the 'surreal' namespace.

steps.title: Create Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for SurrealDB, which is necessary to configure the database instance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: my-surrealdb
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> Creates a SurrealDB instance with one replica.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: my-surrealdb
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify SurrealDB is Ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the SurrealDB pods to ensure that they are running and ready to accept connections.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n surreal

```

> Verify that the SurrealDB pod is in 'Running' state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step removes the SurrealDB installation from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all data stored in SurrealDB.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surreal

kubectl delete namespace surreal

```

> Uninstalls the SurrealDB release.
> Deletes the namespace and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl cp surrealdb/my-surrealdb-0:/data /backup/data

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --version 3.0.2

kubectl get pods -n surreal

```

> Backup your data before the upgrade.
> Upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgraded SurrealDB pod is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n surreal' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB service is not reachable, possibly due to network policies or service misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running with 'kubectl get svc -n surreal' and check network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU/Memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB pod is unable to start due to insufficient resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the SurrealDB deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CustomResourceDefinition already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD for SurrealDB is already installed in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check existing CRDs with 'kubectl get crd' and remove the existing one if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new indexing features and improved query performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n surreal'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
